seoTitle: Fountaindale vs Four Mile Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fountaindale and Four Mile Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fountaindale vs Four Mile Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fountaindale and Four Mile Creek.
heading: Fountaindale vs Four Mile Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fountaindale, New South Wales and Four Mile Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Four Mile Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Fountaindale (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fountaindale or Four Mile Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fountaindale has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Four Mile Creek scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fountaindale scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Four Mile Creek scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fountaindale or Four Mile Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Four Mile Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
